one_liner: NVDA -1.25% to $197.58 on the Meta cloud selloff — relatively resilient vs peers (MU -10%, INTC -9%), with low-volume decline suggesting institutional holders are not capitulating.

price_action.intraday_story: NVDA dipped to $193.45 early, recovered to $199.85 mid-day, then settled at $197.58. The 0.80x below-average volume on a day when MU lost 10% and INTC lost 9% signals that NVIDIA holders view Meta's cloud plans as a second-order threat rather than a direct competitor. NVIDIA supplies both Meta's AI infrastructure AND any future excess-compute monetization business.

news_catalyst.headline: Meta cloud announcement hits AI chip sector; NVDA declines just 1.25% vs MU -10.57% and INTC -9.03%
news_catalyst.summary: NVIDIA's comparative resilience today is the story. Meta's cloud plan is actually double-edged for NVDA: if Meta sells compute time, it still needs NVDA's GPUs to run it. The incremental concern is whether Meta's announcement signals the hyperscaler AI buildout is near peak — which would slow incremental H200/B200 orders. But NVIDIA's backlog remains years long and competitors (AMD, Intel) are still nowhere close to matching H100/B200 performance. The market is differentiating: memory (commodity) gets sold hard; GPU leaders (duopolies) get a pass.

technical.summary: NVDA has been consolidating between $189-201 for the past week. The 0.80x volume on today's mild decline is the most constructive data point — if institutions were concerned about Meta's cloud plans undermining the AI chip thesis, this would have been a 1.5x+ volume distribution day. Instead, it's a low-volume drift lower. DCF shows $333.93 base intrinsic value vs $197.58 (40.8% MoS) — the largest-MoS feasible DCF in the chip names. The bear DCF at $192.02 is just below current levels, providing a natural support floor.

wyckoff_narrative: NVDA is in consolidation without a clear Wyckoff phase. The stock needs to break above $200 on volume 1.5x+ to initiate a new markup phase, or test the $189.80 June 29 low to potentially establish a Spring setup. Today's action is inconclusive — neither distribution nor accumulation signal. The 40.8% DCF margin of safety at current levels provides fundamental support for a patient holder.

valuation.summary: DCF shows $333.93 base (40.8% MoS), $192.02 bear (just below current price), $518.73 bull. The bear DCF near current levels is important — it means NVDA is already near its fundamental bear-case value, limiting downside on a pure DCF basis.

key_strengths: AI GPU monopoly position with H200/B200
key_concerns: Meta cloud plans introduce second-order hyperscaler capex risk
Stock has had massive multiple expansion — any growth miss is severely punished
fundamentals.summary: NVIDIA's fundamentals are the strongest in the semiconductor sector. 85% YoY revenue growth with a $91B Q2 guide. Meta's cloud announcement is a minor headwind at most.

risks: Break below $189.80 (June 29 low) on volume >1.3x invites test of $180 support
Meta cloud announcement creates a narrative overhang that could weigh on multiple expansion
Q2 FY2027 earnings reaction (guidance vs expectations) is the key binary event
